Title: A network of jasmonate-responsive bHLH factors modulate monoterpenoid indole alkaloid biosynthesis in Catharanthus roseus

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supports repression of JA-responsive MIA regulators through JAZ-associated corepressor function
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrNINJA was cloned as a Catharanthus co-repressor homolog. 2. It interacted with all tested CrJAZs except CrJAZ8. 3. The data support a CrJAZ-NINJA-TPL-like repression module in JA-controlled MIA regulation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: potential target to modulate repressor strength in combination with JAZ/COI1 engineering
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TPL involvement was inferred from canonical NINJA function but not directly tested in this paper

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: likely negative regulator in JA signaling linked to MIA biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: yeast two-hybrid; tobacco protoplast-based two-hybrid; localization analysis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrJAZ3 is a JA-inducible nuclear JAZ protein. 2. It interacted with CrCOI1 in a coronatine-dependent manner and with CrMYC2 and CrNINJA. 3. CrJAZ3 overexpression lines were severely stunted, but detailed MIA output analysis was not presented.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: functional negative regulator candidate, but strong developmental penalty complicates use
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although part of the JAZ network, it was not phenotypically characterized for MIA accumulation in detail in the main results
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: all CrJAZ3 overexpression hairy root lines were severely stunted

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supports SCFCOI1 complex assembly for JA-mediated de-repression of MIA biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: protein interaction/complex assembly evidence in supplementary data
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrASK1 was identified as a Catharanthus SCF component. 2. It supported formation of the SCFCOI1 complex implicated in JA signaling upstream of MIA biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: supporting component rather than main experimental focus

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supports SCFCOI1 complex assembly for JA signaling
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: protein interaction/complex assembly evidence in supplementary data
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrCUL1 was identified as a Catharanthus SCF complex scaffold component. 2. It participates in the SCFCOI1 complex acting upstream of JA-controlled MIA biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: supporting component rather than main experimental focus

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supports SCFCOI1 E3 ligase complex assembly for JA-mediated control of MIA biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: protein interaction/complex assembly evidence in supplementary data
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrRBX1 was identified as an SCF complex component in Catharanthus. 2. It supports the SCFCOI1 machinery upstream of JA-responsive MIA regulation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: supporting component rather than main experimental focus

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: monoterpene indole alkaloids; catharanthine; tabersonine; ajmalicine
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: represses monoterpene indole alkaloid biosynthesis and reduces catharanthine, tabersonine, and ajmalicine accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: JA-induced passive repressor that antagonizes CrMYC2 output and limits MIA accumulation by suppressing CrMYC2 target genes
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: transcriptional regulation of the indole/MIA branch, especially CrMYC2-dependent activation of ORCA3 and downstream MIA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional repression by competitive promoter binding
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: represses ORCA3 and STR; can reduce CrMYC2 transcript in stable hairy roots; does not significantly affect BIS1
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: methyl jasmonate; CrMYC2 activation; BIS1-dependent indirect activation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: MeJA inducible; moderately higher transcript in epidermal cells (nearly veinless) than IPAP-enriched veins
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: MeJA inducible; moderately higher transcript in epidermal cells (nearly veinless) than IPAP-enriched veins
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient overexpression in Catharanthus protoplasts; promoter transactivation assay in tobacco protoplasts; stable overexpression in hairy roots; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; EMSA; promoter mutagenesis; yeast two-hybrid
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RMT1 was identified as a JA-responsive bHLH factor co-expressed with CrMYC2. 2. CrMYC2 directly activated the RMT1 promoter, while BIS1 increased RMT1 expression indirectly. 3. RMT1 repressed ORCA3 and STR expression and decreased catharanthine, tabersonine, and ajmalicine accumulation in hairy roots. 4. RMT1 bound the same T/G-box motifs in the ORCA3 promoter as CrMYC2 and antagonized CrMYC2 in a dosage-dependent manner.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: participates in a negative feedback loop downstream of CrMYC2 and in crosstalk with BIS1 cascade
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: no CrMYC2-RMT1 heterodimer detected; interacts with CrJAZ1 in yeast
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: pharmaceutically valuable defense alkaloids; some MIAs are cytotoxic and associated with anticancer activity
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: RMT1 is a negative engineering target; reducing its activity may help relieve repression on CrMYC2 targets, but flux balance and toxicity risks must be considered
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: RMT1 lacks a known repressor domain and likely acts as a passive repressor through competition for T/G-box binding sites rather than direct interaction with CrMYC2

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: F-box protein; COI1 jasmonate co-receptor; SCF complex component
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: monoterpene indole alkaloids; catharanthine; ajmalicine; tabersonine; vindoline
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes jasmonate-induced monoterpene indole alkaloid biosynthesis; silencing decreases alkaloids, inducible overexpression increases MIA accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: sits atop the JA-responsive regulatory network and is crucial for signal perception that coordinately de-represses CrMYC2 and BIS1/BIS2 cascades
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: JA signal perception and de-repression of transcriptional cascades controlling indole and iridoid branches of MIA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: ligand-dependent JAZ recognition and SCF-mediated de-repression
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: promotes expression of CrMYC2, BIS1, BIS2, ORCA3, RMT1, G10H and other JA-responsive MIA pathway genes indirectly by JAZ turnover
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CrJAZ1; CrJAZ3; CrJAZ10; CrASK1; CrCUL1; CrRBX1
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: jasmonate perception; coronatine as JA-Ile mimic; dexamethasone in inducible overexpression system
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: expressed in both IPAP-enriched veins and epidermal cells with no significant difference reported
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expressed in both IPAP-enriched veins and epidermal cells with no significant difference reported
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS in planta; RNAi in hairy roots; inducible overexpression in hairy roots; Arabidopsis coi1-30 complementation; yeast two-hybrid; tobacco protoplast-based two-hybrid; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrCOI1 is a functional ortholog of Arabidopsis COI1 and forms an SCFCOI1 complex. 2. CrCOI1 interacts with selected CrJAZ proteins in a coronatine-dependent manner. 3. Silencing CrCOI1 reduced expression of key regulators and lowered catharanthine, ajmalicine, tabersonine, and vindoline. 4. Inducible CrCOI1 overexpression elevated CrMYC2/BIS1/BIS2 cascades and increased MIA accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: acts upstream in JA-triggered de-repression; not described as direct feedback regulator
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: SCF^COI1 complex with CrASK1, CrCUL1, and CrRBX1
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 100 µM MeJA; coronatine; 30 µM dexamethasone for inducible expression
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: pharmaceutically valuable defense alkaloids with cytotoxic/anticancer relevance
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: constitutive overexpression caused stunted hairy roots, poor survival, and JA hypersensitivity; RNAi lines showed improved root growth
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: inducible or controlled CrCOI1 activation is promising to boost MIA production, but constitutive activation causes growth penalties and JA hypersensitivity
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CrCOI1 acts upstream of both CrMYC2- and BIS-dependent cascades and coordinates balanced pathway activation

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: monoterpene indole alkaloids; catharanthine; ajmalicine; tabersonine
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: represses MIA pathway regulators and decreases MIA biosynthetic output
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: core JA signaling repressor that suppresses both CrMYC2 and BIS1 cascades until relieved by CrCOI1-mediated signaling
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: transcriptional repression of MIA biosynthesis through CrMYC2 and BIS1 pathways
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: protein-protein interaction-mediated corepression; de-repressed by COI1-dependent signaling
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: represses ORCA3 and G10H; overexpression reduces CrMYC2, ORCA3, BIS1, and G10H expression in hairy roots
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: jasmonate; CrMYC2-induced transcription; coronatine promotes CrCOI1 interaction
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: JA inducible; present in both epidermal and IPAP-enriched tissues
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: JA inducible; present in both epidermal and IPAP-enriched tissues
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient overexpression in protoplasts; stable overexpression in hairy roots; yeast two-hybrid; tobacco protoplast-based two-hybrid; qRT-PCR
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrJAZ1 is induced by CrMYC2 and by JA signaling. 2. CrJAZ1 physically interacts with CrCOI1 in a coronatine-dependent manner and with CrMYC2 and CrNINJA. 3. CrJAZ1 overexpression represses ORCA3 and G10H in protoplasts and represses CrMYC2/BIS1 pathway outputs in hairy roots. 4. CrJAZ1 therefore functions as a negative regulator within the JA-MIA network.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: part of a negative feedback loop downstream of CrMYC2 and JA signaling
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: CrJAZ1-CrMYC2 complex; probable BIS1-CrJAZ1 repressor complex in plant cells; CrJAZ1-NINJA-TPL type repressor complex inferred
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: negative regulator and candidate repression target for pathway engineering, though complete removal may disturb pathway balance
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CrJAZ1 is one of the best-supported JAZ proteins in this study because both COI1 interaction and functional repression were demonstrated

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: represses CrMYC2/BIS1-related MIA gene expression when overexpressed
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: acts as a negative regulator of both transcriptional cascades and may be relatively stable because no CrCOI1 interaction was detected
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: overexpression reduces CrMYC2, ORCA3, BIS1, and G10H expression in hairy roots; represses BIS1 transactivation in protoplasts
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: jasmonate; CrMYC2-induced transcription reported for CrJAZ2
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: stable overexpression in hairy roots; yeast two-hybrid; protoplast interaction/transactivation assays; qRT-PCR
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrJAZ2 is a JA-inducible nuclear JAZ protein. 2. It interacts with CrMYC2 and CrNINJA, but not detectably with CrCOI1 under the assay conditions. 3. CrJAZ2 suppresses BIS1 transactivation in plant cells and lowers MIA-related gene expression when overexpressed in hairy roots.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: part of JA negative feedback repression
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: CrJAZ2-NINJA corepressor complex inferred; probable BIS1-associated repressor complex in plant cells
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJA; coronatine assay showed no CrCOI1 interaction
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: potential negative target for engineering, but stability outside COI1 control may complicate manipulation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Despite conserved Jas motif features, CrJAZ2 failed to interact with CrCOI1 in this study, implying noncanonical determinants of receptor binding

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: represses MIA-related gene expression when overexpressed
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: negative regulator likely more stable because it lacks canonical COI1-interaction features and was not recruited by CrCOI1 in this study
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: transcriptional repression of JA-responsive MIA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: overexpression reduces CrMYC2, ORCA3, BIS1, and G10H expression in hairy roots
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: stable overexpression in hairy roots; yeast two-hybrid; localization analysis; qRT-PCR
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrJAZ8 is a JA-inducible nuclear JAZ protein. 2. It failed to interact with CrCOI1 and CrNINJA in this study, likely because of a noncanonical Jas degron. 3. Overexpression of CrJAZ8 still repressed CrMYC2/BIS1 pathway outputs in hairy roots, supporting a negative role in MIA regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: part of JA negative feedback repression
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: exact corepressor complex unresolved; unlike most CrJAZs, no CrNINJA interaction detected
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJA; coronatine assay showed no CrCOI1 interaction
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: important negative node because of potential COI1-insensitive stability, but mechanism may differ from canonical JAZs
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: absence of a canonical Jas degron probably hinders CrCOI1 interaction

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: represses MIA regulatory cascades and lowers pathway gene expression when overexpressed
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: negative regulator of MIA biosynthesis that can be de-repressed through coronatine/JA-dependent interaction with CrCOI1, though interaction is weaker than CrJAZ1
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: protein-protein interaction-mediated repression relieved by COI1-ligand interaction
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: overexpression reduces CrMYC2, ORCA3, BIS1, and G10H expression in hairy roots; represses BIS1 transactivation in plant cells
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: stable overexpression in hairy roots; yeast two-hybrid; tobacco protoplast-based two-hybrid; qRT-PCR
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrJAZ10 is a JA-inducible nuclear JAZ protein. 2. It interacts with CrMYC2 and CrNINJA and shows weaker coronatine-dependent interaction with CrCOI1. 3. Overexpression of CrJAZ10 represses both CrMYC2- and BIS1-dependent outputs in hairy roots.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: part of JA negative feedback repression
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: CrJAZ10-NINJA corepressor complex inferred; probable BIS1-associated repressor complex in plant cells
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: possible repression target for engineering balanced MIA enhancement
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: weak CrCOI1 interaction may reflect divergence in the Jas motif, including absence of a typical X5PY motif

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: activates indole/MIA pathway genes but alone does not increase overall MIA accumulation because of negative feedback and flux imbalance
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major JA-responsive activator of ORCA3 and indole-branch genes, yet constrained by JAZ and RMT1-mediated repression
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: transcriptional activation of indole branch and MIA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation via promoter binding to T/G-box motifs
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates ORCA3, TDC, STR, CrJAZ1, CrJAZ2, and RMT1; represses BIS1 target outputs such as G10H and 7DLH indirectly
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: ORCA3; TDC; STR; CrJAZ1; CrJAZ2; RMT1
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: jasmonate signaling through CrCOI1; repression by CrJAZ proteins and RMT1
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: moderately higher in epidermal cells (nearly veinless) than in IPAP-enriched veins; JA responsive
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: moderately higher in epidermal cells (nearly veinless) than in IPAP-enriched veins; JA responsive
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient overexpression in mesophyll protoplasts; promoter transactivation assay; EMSA; yeast two-hybrid; expression analysis in CrCOI1 and JAZ perturbation lines
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrMYC2 activates ORCA3, TDC and STR and induces CrJAZ1/2 and RMT1. 2. It binds T/G-box elements in the ORCA3 promoter. 3. Its activity is repressed by CrJAZ proteins and antagonized by RMT1. 4. CrMYC2 overexpression alone suppresses BIS1 target outputs, highlighting flux-balancing crosstalk.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: central node in JA-triggered negative feedback through induction of JAZs and RMT1
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: forms repressor complexes with CrJAZ proteins; no detectable interaction with RMT1
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: single-gene upregulation is insufficient; should be combined with relief of repressors and balancing of iridoid branch flux
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although previously known, CrMYC2 is a core experimental gene in this paper because its targets, repression, and network position were directly tested

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: activates iridoid branch genes but alone does not increase overall MIA accumulation because it induces repression of CrMYC2 targets and causes flux imbalance
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major JA-responsive activator of iridoid-branch genes, coordinated with but antagonistically linked to CrMYC2 pathway
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: transcriptional activation of iridoid branch supplying MIA precursors
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation, probably through N-box/G-box-containing promoters
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates G10H and 7DLH; represses ORCA3, STR and possibly TDC indirectly; induces RMT1 indirectly
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: jasmonate signaling through CrCOI1; repression by CrJAZ proteins
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: present in both tissues, relatively associated with IPAP-enriched veins compared with CrMYC2/RMT1; JA responsive
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: present in both tissues, relatively associated with IPAP-enriched veins compared with CrMYC2/RMT1; JA responsive
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient overexpression in mesophyll protoplasts; protoplast transactivation assays; expression analysis in CrCOI1 and JAZ perturbation lines
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BIS1 activates iridoid pathway genes such as G10H and 7DLH. 2. BIS1 overexpression represses CrMYC2 target genes including ORCA3 and STR, demonstrating negative crosstalk. 3. BIS1 induces RMT1 expression indirectly, and JAZ proteins suppress BIS1 transactivation in plant cells. 4. Balanced activation of BIS1 with CrMYC2 is necessary for MIA production.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: participates in crosstalk-mediated negative loop with CrMYC2 via JAZs and RMT1
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: probable BIS1-CrJAZ repressor complexes in plant cells; direct in vitro confirmation unsuccessful
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: single-gene activation is insufficient; coordinated engineering with CrMYC2 and reduced repression is more promising
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: BIS1 self-activation hindered direct yeast two-hybrid testing with JAZs, so its interaction with JAZs is inferred from plant-cell assays

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: positively associated with JA-induced MIA pathway output downstream of CrCOI1
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: component of the BIS-dependent JA cascade whose expression responds strongly to CrCOI1 perturbation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: expression analysis in CrCOI1-VIGS, CrCOI1-RNAi, and inducible CrCOI1 overexpression lines
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BIS2 expression decreased when CrCOI1 was silenced and increased when CrCOI1 was induced. 2. These data place BIS2 downstream of CrCOI1 in the JA-responsive MIA regulatory network. 3. The paper treats BIS2 as part of the balanced BIS cascade needed for MIA production.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: part of JA-responsive regulatory cascade, but no direct feedback mechanism tested here
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: likely useful in combinatorial engineering with BIS1/CrMYC2 rather than alone
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: BIS2 was not functionally dissected in as much detail as BIS1 in this paper, but was directly measured as part of the core network
